query: Please go ahead and show me the absolute cheapest business class option, even if it has a connection. I’m fine with a one-stop if it saves money.

state.history_summary:
assistant: Hi! How can I help you today?
user: The user checks stored gift card and certificate balances, then asks to modify an upcoming round trip to the cheapest business-class option on the same dates and route.
assistant: The assistant retrieves the existing reservation from the user account.
assistant: The assistant searches both direct and one-stop outbound and return flight options in business class for the original dates and route.
assistant: With multiple direct and connecting combinations now available in context, the assistant asks whether the user wants the absolute cheapest option even if it involves a connection or would rather prioritize direct flights if the price difference is small.
user: The user explicitly chooses the absolute cheapest business-class option, including one-stop itineraries if that saves money.

candidate_answers.text: Understood. I’ll compare the available outbound and return business-class combinations and identify the absolute cheapest round-trip option for you.

The flight search results are already in hand and the user has clarified the optimization target, so the grounded next move is to compute which combination is cheapest before presenting or booking anything.

provenance.source_type: manual annotation from real tau2 episode
provenance.decision_rule: Choose the point after all direct and one-stop search results are already visible and the user defines the optimization objective as absolute cheapest total business-class itinerary.
provenance.scoring_method: Manual one-step proxy scoring from the visible state. calculate is highest because the assistant now needs to compute totals across candidate combinations to identify the lowest-cost round trip before asking for booking confirmation.
notes: this sample diversifies best_action with an arithmetic tool rather than lookup or mutation
search tools are no longer best because the relevant option sets have already been retrieved into context
the later assistant arithmetic mistake in the raw episode does not change the normative best next action at this decision point
